description_long: This house features a steep blue gable roof with visible wooden beams and white plaster walls, reminiscent of traditional Tudor architecture. The front facade is marked by a prominent arched wooden door framed by red brick columns and a small overhanging roof. A large brick chimney rises along one side, accompanied by a gray vent pipe, adding to the rustic charm. The windows are modest and symmetrically placed, with wooden frames and no elaborate decoration. The structure sits on a grassy plot, suggesting a suburban or village setting. The overall appearance is cozy and inviting, with a blend of brick, wood, and plaster materials.
description: A quaint Tudor-style house with a steep blue gable roof, exposed wooden beams, red brick chimney, and an arched wooden door set in a grassy yard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched wooden door, red brick columns, and overhanging roof detail.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large brick chimney, blue shingled roof, and a small window.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical wooden beam pattern and plain plaster wall with no visible entry.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another window, brick chimney, and the continuation of the blue roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched wooden door framed by red brick columns, small overhanging roof, no porch or steps, simple and rustic.
